I'm trying to stay focused while working from home. Do you have any simple tips that actually help?

Yes—keep it simple: set one clear task for the next 25–30 minutes, put your phone out of reach, and work in a tidy spot if you can. Taking short breaks between focus sessions also helps prevent burnout. If distractions keep popping up, write them down instead of acting on them right away, then come back to your main task.

I have a sore throat and a mild cough. Any simple home remedies I can try?

You can try warm tea with honey, stay well hydrated, gargle with warm salt water, and use a humidifier if the air is dry. Resting your voice may also help. If you develop a high fever, trouble breathing, severe pain, or symptoms that last more than several days, it’s a good idea to contact a medical professional.

I'm trying to stay focused while working from home. Do you have a few practical tips?

Yes — a few simple habits can help a lot:

1. Set a clear start time so work feels intentional.
2. Pick 1–3 top priorities for the day.
3. Work in short focused blocks, like 25–50 minutes.
4. Keep your phone out of reach during focus time.
5. Use a dedicated workspace, even if it's just one corner.
6. Take real breaks: stand up, stretch, get water.
7. End the day with a quick shutdown routine so work doesn’t spill into your evening.

If you want, I can also suggest a simple daily work-from-home routine.

I keep waking up groggy even after 8 hours of sleep. Any general tips to improve sleep quality?

A few general things can help: keep a consistent sleep/wake time, limit caffeine late in the day, reduce screens for 30–60 minutes before bed, keep your room cool and dark, and avoid heavy meals or alcohol close to bedtime. If you snore loudly, wake up often, or feel tired for weeks despite enough sleep, it may be worth talking to a healthcare professional.

I’ve been feeling distracted working from home lately. Do you have any simple tips to stay focused during the day?

A few simple things can help: set a clear start time, pick 1–3 priority tasks for the day, and work in short blocks like 25–45 minutes with brief breaks. It also helps to keep your phone out of reach, use a dedicated workspace if possible, and separate work from home tasks by writing them down instead of switching immediately. If you want, I can also suggest a sample work-from-home routine.

{"owner":"vercel","repo":"next.js","issue_number":128,"title":"Image optimization fails on external SVG assets","state":"open","author":"jdoe","labels":["bug","image","triage"],"created_at":"2024-02-11T09:14:22Z","updated_at":"2024-02-13T16:48:05Z","body":"When using next/image with an external SVG URL, the optimizer returns a 400 error instead of serving the asset. Repro included in the linked repository.","comments":[{"author":"maintainer1","created_at":"2024-02-11T12:03:10Z","body":"Thanks for the repro. We can confirm this on canary as well."},{"author":"contributor7","created_at":"2024-02-12T08:41:55Z","body":"Looks related to MIME type handling in the image loader path."},{"author":"jdoe","created_at":"2024-02-13T16:48:05Z","body":"Confirmed a workaround is to bypass optimization for SVGs, but native support would still be helpful."}]}

Issue #128 in vercel/next.js is open: “Image optimization fails on external SVG assets.” It’s labeled bug, image, and triage. The thread confirms the problem on canary, suggests MIME type handling as the likely cause, and notes a current workaround of bypassing optimization for SVGs.

I keep waking up around 3 a.m. and then can't fall back asleep. Any simple tips to improve my sleep?

A few simple things can help: keep a consistent sleep/wake time, avoid caffeine late in the day, dim screens and bright lights 1–2 hours before bed, and keep your room cool and dark. If you wake up and can’t fall back asleep after about 20 minutes, get out of bed and do something quiet and relaxing until you feel sleepy again. If this keeps happening for weeks or affects daytime functioning, it may be worth talking to a doctor.
